title: podman generate kube defaults to no restartPolicy, meaning restartPolicy=always
body:
```
podman version  
Client:       Podman Engine 
Version:      4.3.1
API Version:  4.3.1                                                                                                                                          
Go Version:   go1.19.2                                                                                                                                      
Built:        Fri Nov 11 15:01:27 2022
```

/kind bug
The default kube manifest created by podman generate kube has no restartPolicy set. When creating a systemd unit file from this pod, podman warns you that your pod's restartPolicy is set to always, the default when undefined.



**Describe the results you received:**
```
podman generate systemd --files --name nginx-pod                                                                                  WARN[0000] Container 7691df68e80cfb2402b9394fa857b4480a69a43027d0883c0071916abde419be has restart policy "always" which can lead to issues on shutdown: consider recreating the container without a restart policy and use systemd's restart mechanism instead 
```
**Describe the results you expected:**
Expect the default to be more sensible interpretation by podman generate kube or podman generate systemd, one of these should default to a policy that aligns better with controlling pods via systemd.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version  
Client:       Podman Engine 
Version:      4.3.1
API Version:  4.3.1                                                                                                                                          
Go Version:   go1.19.2                                                                                                                                      
Built:        Fri Nov 11 15:01:27 2022
```



**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman` or `brew info podman`):**

```
podman-4.3.1-1.fc37.x86_64 
```



Yes

comments.body:
@mbach04, I recommend using the [systemd template](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-generate-systemd.1.html#kubernetes-integration) for running K8s YAML in systemd.

For your specific case, I am not sure what Podman could do.  Kubernetes defaults to restart=always so `podman kube play` is using that to remain compatible.  Using that in systemd does not always make sense, so Podman is warning about it.  You can always change the policy via `generate systemd --restart-policy=on-failure`, for instance.

Thanks for the speedy response. I appreciate the info. I do think however, there's something missing in the user experience here, though I can't say what the right approach is to fix it. Podman has this great ability to run pods and to run kube yaml, which is super helpful. It seems odd to me that the defaults don't align with the least friction possible to do this.

I have tried the systemd template approach after running some kube yaml. The template generated fails to stop and start the pods unfortunately (maybe a separate bug/issue I should submit). Additionally, the docs for the template approach are incredibly terse. Some more extended examples would be incredibly helpful.

>  The template generated fails to stop and start the pods unfortunately (maybe a separate bug/issue I should submit).

I'd love to know more about that.  If you find the time and a reproducer, would you open another issue?

> I do think however, there's something missing in the user experience here, though I can't say what the right approach is to fix it.

Since Podman is already warning, the message should probably point to a man page explaining the systemd template.

> Some more extended examples would be incredibly helpful.

I will take a look at the man page and try to elaborate more on the template.

Thanks for your input, @mbach04!

> Since Podman is already warning, the message should probably point to a man page explaining the systemd template.

That won't work as I had intended.  I will keep thinking.
Circling back here. I embraced the kube systemd template approach. It actually worked out well for my purposes. Thanks!

> Circling back here. I embraced the kube systemd template approach. It actually worked out well for my purposes. Thanks!

Thanks, I am glad you like it.  Stay tuned for the Podman 4.4 release early next year.  [Quadlet](https://github.com/containers/quadlet/) is now part of Podman and added support for running K8s YAML as well. This will be the recommended way for running Podman workloads in systemd starting with the 4.4 release.
